title_en: Author Biography and the Mu'tazili Tradition
content_id:
Abu al-Qasim Mahmud ibn Umar az-Zamakhsyari lahir di Zamakhsyar, sebuah desa di wilayah Khwarazm di Asia Tengah, pada tahun 467 H (1075 M). Beliau menjadi salah satu filolog, ahli tata bahasa, dan ulama al-Quran paling brilian dalam periode Islam klasik, mendapat gelar Jar Allah (Tetangga Allah) karena tinggalnya yang lama di dekat Ka'bah di Makkah. Kecerdasan intelektualnya diakui secara luas di berbagai garis doktrin, bahkan oleh para ulama yang sangat tidak setuju dengan komitmen teologisnya.

Az-Zamakhsyari adalah seorang Mu'tazilah yang berkomitmen — penganut mazhab teologi rasionalis yang menekankan keadilan ilahi, kehendak bebas manusia, dan sifat ciptaan al-Quran. Mazhab Mu'tazilah telah menjadi kekuatan dominan dalam teologi Islam selama abad ketiga Islam dan setelahnya, meskipun menghadapi oposisi yang semakin terorganisir dari kaum tradisionalis Hanbali dan kemudian dari mazhab-mazhab Asy'ari dan Maturidi. Pada masa az-Zamakhsyari, Mu'tazilisme adalah posisi teologis minoritas, tetapi yang masih dipertahankan dengan penuh semangat oleh tradisi ulama yang serius secara intelektual.

Tafsirnya, Al-Kashshaf an Haqa'iq Ghawamid at-Tanzil wa 'Uyun al-Aqawil fi Wujuh at-Ta'wil (Pengungkap Kebenaran-Kebenaran Tersembunyi Wahyu dan Pernyataan-Pernyataan Terbaik tentang Aspek-Aspek Penafsiran), yang dikenal secara universal sebagai Al-Kashshaf, diselesaikan pada tahun 528 H (1134 M) di Makkah. Ini adalah salah satu karya yang paling brilian secara teknis dalam tradisi tafsir.

Al-Kashshaf dirayakan terutama karena analisis retorika dan linguistik bahasa Arabnya. Penguasaan az-Zamakhsyari terhadap bahasa Arab klasik — tata bahasanya, leksikografinya, konvensi sastranya, figur-figur retorisnya — memberinya alat-alat interpretatif yang tidak dimiliki oleh komentator-komentator lain, dan analisis-analisisnya tentang retorika al-Quran menetapkan standar yang tidak pernah sepenuhnya dilampaui oleh ulama-ulama kemudian. Pada saat yang sama, posisi-posisi teologis Mu'tazilahnya mengalir di seluruh karya ini, memerlukan pembacaan kritis yang cermat dari para ulama dengan orientasi teologis lain.

Beliau meninggal di Jurjaniyya di Khwarazm pada tahun 538 H (1144 M). Meskipun heterodoksinya secara teologis menurut standar Sunni mainstream, beasiswa linguistiknya menjadikan Al-Kashshaf sebagai teks tetap dalam kanon tafsir.
content_en:
Abu al-Qasim Mahmud ibn Umar az-Zamakhshari was born in Zamakhshar, a village in the Khwarazm region of Central Asia, in 467 AH (1075 CE). He became one of the most brilliant philologists, grammarians, and Quran scholars of the classical Islamic period, earning the title Jar Allah (Neighbor of Allah) for his extended residence near the Kaaba in Makkah. His intellectual gifts were widely recognized across doctrinal lines, even by scholars who sharply disagreed with his theological commitments.

Az-Zamakhshari was a committed Mu'tazili — an adherent of the rationalist theological school that emphasized divine justice, human free will, and the created nature of the Quran. The Mu'tazilite school had been a dominant force in Islamic theology during the third Islamic century and thereafter, though it faced increasingly organized opposition from Hanbali traditionalists and later from the Ash'ari and Maturidi schools. By az-Zamakhshari's time, Mu'tazilism was a minority theological position, but one still vigorously maintained by a tradition of intellectually serious scholars.

His tafsir, Al-Kashshaf an Haqa'iq Ghawamid at-Tanzil wa 'Uyun al-Aqawil fi Wujuh at-Ta'wil (The Revealer of the Truths of the Obscurities of Revelation and the Best Statements about the Aspects of Interpretation), universally known as Al-Kashshaf, was completed in 528 AH (1134 CE) in Makkah. It is among the most technically brilliant works in the tafsir tradition.

Al-Kashshaf is celebrated above all for its Arabic rhetorical and linguistic analysis. Az-Zamakhshari's mastery of classical Arabic — its grammar, its lexicography, its literary conventions, its rhetorical figures — gave him interpretive tools that other commentators lacked, and his analyses of Quranic rhetoric set a standard that later scholars have never fully surpassed. At the same time, his Mu'tazili theological positions run throughout the work, requiring careful critical reading from scholars of other theological orientations.

He died in Jurjaniyya in Khwarazm in 538 AH (1144 CE). Despite his theological heterodoxy by mainstream Sunni standards, his linguistic scholarship made Al-Kashshaf a permanent fixture of the tafsir canon.
